我开发的 Claude Code 客户端 Codepilot 更新 0.15 版本

彻底重构了模型的添加和使用逻辑，现在更像 OpenCode 

你可以直接在聊天窗选择所有已经添加的模型，不需要回到设置页面

设置页面内置了很多常见的 Code Plan，只需填写 Key 就行 https://t.co/SpVUYUkFhn

Difference between today and past technology booms; Railroad barons in the 1880s did not think that the next mile of track might bring about the Eschaton.

Telco executives in 1999 did not expect that another line of fiber optic cable would possibly usher in the world-to-come.
You avoid commoditization by immanentizing the eschaton.

Claude Sonnet 4.6 on @Vibecodeapp is that good. https://t.co/VPdLtzsTn4

We've solved vector design with AI ✨

@QuiverAI makes it incredibly easy to generate SVGs from text or images.

When you treat visuals as code, you get a much more composable output and can do precise edits.

You also get to watch the model sketch in real-time 👇 https://t.co/t4JN2tllYy

OpenAI 编程助手 Codex 刚刚推出了 0.105.0 版本

被用户称为"史上最大单次更新"

最抢眼的新功能是语音听写：在终端界面里按住空格键就能说话，AI 自动把你的语音转成文字指令。

子代理系统大改：AI 团队协作：

另一个重量级更新是子代理（Subagents）系统的全面重构。 https://t.co/qsoIs1FkFh

其他实用改进：

除了两个大功能，还有一堆"用了才知道爽"的小改进：

主题选择器：终端界面支持换主题了，有明暗两种模式适配，代码高亮和 diff 颜色都重新优化过

防休眠：Codex 运行时可以阻止 Linux 和 Windows 系统进入休眠（跑长任务时终于不怕电脑自己睡着了）

Plan Mode
详细内容：https://t.co/kEuvyUavFb https://t.co/hcqxVk5dkU

黑客利用  Claude 黑掉整个墨西哥政府

窃取了 150GB 墨西哥政府数据 
1.95 亿纳税人记录、选民档案、公务员密码...

整个过程只使用了一台电脑+Claude、GPT月费会员

以色列网络安全公司 Gambit Security 的调查报告，曝出了一起震动AI圈的事件：

一名身份不明的黑客，通过"越狱"Anthropic 的 AI https://t.co/7b2lHNamyr

这件事最让人细思恐极的地方，不是黑客有多厉害，而是他的手法有多简单。

整个攻击的起点，就是跟 Claude 聊天。

> claude 最初拒绝了
> "这违反了 AI 安全准则" 
> 黑客一直换着话术问 ：把请求重新包装成了一个"漏洞赏金项目"
> claude："好的，我会帮忙"
>直接扔给它一份详细的攻击剧本 https://t.co/Vz8Erfa0JF

当 Claude 在某些任务上碰到限制时，黑客没有停下来——他切换到了 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT，用它来完成横向移动（从一个被攻破的系统跳到另一个系统）和规避检测。

两个 AI 工具联合作战，一个负责侦察和初始攻击，一个负责深入渗透和隐蔽行动。 https://t.co/5LB8ukoccw

Anthropic 表示已经调查了 Gambit Security 的报告，中断了相关攻击活动，并封禁了涉事账号。

而墨西哥政府则说："近几个月没有发现任何入侵或未授权访问" 😁

详细内容：https://t.co/I7qjJSw2fE https://t.co/I0GOask4ez

To grok is to drink a thing so deeply that you become it.

理解的终点不是「我懂了」，而是「我就是它」。

比往年机会都多少多，还是有很多新机会的。

Anthropic 宣布收购西雅图 AI 创业公司 Vercept 

Vercept 的产品叫 Vy，一个桌面 AI 助手。它能跟着你学会如何操作电脑...

具体怎么用呢？比如你每天要做一个重复工作：打开浏览器查数据 → 复制到 Excel → 整理格式 → 发邮件给同事。你自己操作一遍，Vy 在旁边"看着"，学会了。

这里面最牛的是 https://t.co/xrAuUEfm86

Vercept 的产品 Vy 将在 30 天内关闭（3 月 25 日），现有用户会被引导转向 Claude。

详细报道：https://t.co/lai2cSJXVk

开启方式：在配置文件里加上：

[features]
voice_transcription = true

也可以让codex 帮你

先升级，然后让codex 帮你开启，退出重新登录即可
So cute! love seeing all your experiments with the prompt
RT @0xJiuJitsuJerry: https://t.co/Ly2chmHPi1
RT @ArtifxAI: Visions. 👁️ ✨ https://t.co/blRAXynrLU
What could it mean?

Has anyone actually benchmarked AI ability with any of the default knowledge work skills shipping with Claude Cowork?

Does it increase GDPval scores over default 4.6? (Not GDPval-AA) It seems worth testing for real, given that the market freaks out every time they ship skills.

So math & AI have gone through a journey in recent months from: "WOW AI did it!!! (but on closer examination it didn't)"  to "It did some of the things it said but hallucinated others" to "It did it with caveats" to "It did over half autonomously"

Other fields will look similar.
For more depth, this IEEE piece is good: https://t.co/1dXnhZiWzj

特斯拉和 OpenAI 前人工智能负责人 Andrej Karpathy 表示，人工智能编码代理现在足够可靠，可以胜任复杂的任务.

例如在他的 NVIDIA DGX Spark 上安装软件、对模型进行基准测试、构建终端以及在 30 分钟的自主工作时间内生成报告。

他指出，Claude 和 Qwen3-VL 等模型在 2025 年 12
And this number is greatly underreported. Actual numbers are much worse.
I’m sure that’s about to happen any day now 🤣
Yes
They’re being taught to hate America
Tunnels are so underrated

This paper broke my brain 🤯

Researchers gave Claude a simple question: “I want to wash my car. The car wash is 100 meters away. Should I walk or drive?”

Claude said walk.

Every major LLM said walk.

The correct answer is drive.

The car has to be there. Here’s the wild part: https://t.co/uq1hzhtgc2
Read the full paper here: https://t.co/beaiF6DX8N

Researchers gave Claude a simple question: “I want to wash my car. The car wash is 100 meters…

RT @godofprompt: Everyone's bragging about 200k, 1M, even 10M token context windows in 2026.

Nobody's talking about what actually happens…

“Think step by step.”

That’s outdated.

OpenAI researchers moved beyond it to something called 'Reasoning Scaffolds.'

It forces structured thinking instead of shallow chains.

Works across every major LLM.

Here’s the format you can copy now: https://t.co/OCOQwBhxRt

First, why "think step by step" fails.

It tells the model to think.

It doesn't tell the model how to think.

You get surface-level reasoning dressed up as depth.

Confident-sounding outputs with zero structural logic underneath. https://t.co/pHZvddi7o7

Reasoning Scaffolds fix this by forcing the model through a locked sequence before it answers.

Not "think step by step."

But:

→ Decompose the problem
→ Identify what's known vs. unknown
→ Map dependencies between sub-problems
→ Solve bottom-up
→ Verify against the https://t.co/j7k5Qx6yis

Here's the actual scaffold format you can copy right now:

"Before answering, work through this sequence:

[DECOMPOSE]: Break the problem into its smallest parts
[KNOWN/UNKNOWN]: What information do I have? What's missing?
[DEPENDENCIES]: Which parts must be solved before others? https://t.co/YiPcii9hqi

The difference in output quality is not small.

Standard chain-of-thought on complex reasoning tasks: surface pattern matching that sounds logical.

Reasoning Scaffold on the same task: actual dependency mapping, explicit uncertainty, and self-verification.

The model stops https://t.co/Le4a5DoeKH

Works on GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok.

Any model with strong instruction following.

The scaffold doesn't rely on model-specific tricks.

It exploits the one thing every frontier LLM has: the ability to follow structured procedures when forced to.

Where this matters most:

→ Complex multi-step math
→ Legal or contract analysis
→ Technical debugging
→ Business strategy with competing tradeoffs
→ Anything where "sounds right" isn't good enough

If the task has real consequences, use a scaffold.

Most people are still writing prompts like it's 2023.

"Be concise." "Think step by step." "You are an expert."

The researchers who actually study this moved on.

Structured scaffolds are what's in the internal docs now.

Copy the format. Use it today. The gap between you and

→ Prompts for marketing & business
→ Unlimited custom prompts
→ n8n automations
→ Weekly updates

Start your free trial👇

谷歌前CEO埃里克·施密特：“伟大的创始人都很节俭”

2001年，埃里克加入谷歌担任CEO，当时距离公司完成2500万美元A轮融资仅两年。

他当时与四位工程师共用一间办公室。甚至有一次，其中一位创始人的车坏了，他还得把自己的车借给其中一位。

他认为这种节俭是优秀创始人的共同特质：

大家好，分享今日的收获给大家：

1.  春节假期读书，让我收获最大的一句话是：当你免费使用某项服务（技术）时，如果你不知道公司是怎么赚钱的，你就要小心你自己就是‘商品’。

2.  最近看到推特的 For you 又推荐好多web3的人，很多人的个人简介就是‘搞钱...

"show me a where's waldo set in ancient Venice, but instead of waldo it is an otter wearing a blue striped pilots outfit." https://t.co/MPeB3nTE1w

Nano banana 2, first few attempts at complicated toasting

Much faster, and not perfect but real improvements in text and ability to handle complexity - even getting detailed labels right at a level we haven't seen before (though still issues sometimes) https://t.co/t1FxN5KJ6i
Manus is still not as useful as @openclaw

In 2019 it was somewhat rare and cool to forgo a safe traditional job to be an influencer. 

In 2026 it is safer to be an influencer than to get a traditional job.

The way to distinguish AI generated video right now is don't trust your eyes, trust your ears

But yeah by mid-year it's fully over

RT @godofprompt: Here's why I use Claude a lot:

1. It actually reads your entire document before answering, not just the first paragraph
2…

Nano Banana 2 is out.

I had early access for the past few days, and tested it across a ton of prompts.

It's leveled up for a bunch of use cases - infographics, ads, action shots, even cartoons. And it's crazy fast!

Some styles + prompts you should try 👇 https://t.co/c4aAcgBUTo

1) Infographics

NB Pro was a big upgrade for this - but sometimes the text would get strange or the information wasn't accurate.

NB 2 can generate long, detailed infographics incredibly fast by leveraging Google search.

E.g. "make a guide for Americans riding public transit in https://t.co/Pj5GpBUt8B

2) Action shots

Sports photography has historically been difficult for AI image models - the little details of muscle and physics of movement are challenging.

NB 2 is surprisingly good at this. Some of the shots even looked like ads, so I asked the model to write overlays. https://t.co/XigjgNCcG1

3) Product photography

The model is great at maintaining small details from a reference photo - like the olive oil bottle and the green dress.

IMO, the outputs look more editorial and less AI than previous models.

These prompts were simple - upload one product photo with a https://t.co/Licfwals3q

4) Localization

This model was supposed to be particularly good at rendering real-world locations.

I tested it by asking to generate a shot looking down the aisle of a grocery store in four locations: Tokyo, Mexico City, Paris, and LA.

Look at the signs and the products 👀 https://t.co/B2xswy8lCe

5) Architectural diagrams

NB Pro could make some very cool sketches and diagrams...but unfortunately the measurements were almost always incorrect.

NB 2 actually searches for the correct info and incorporates it in the diagram. To check, you can Google how tall One World Trade https://t.co/ITGb2hJBDK

6) Cartoons

I never got previous image models to work for cartoons beyond a few frames - they would lose the plot.

NB 2 is a big upgrade.

This eight panel cartoon came from a simple prompt: "make a cartoon about Sam Altman's firing and re-hiring at OpenAI" 🤯 https://t.co/zxeY0pdG6A

7) UGC

The model can one-shot TikTok screenshots, which is kind of crazy! And overall looks v realistic for AI UGC.

These images were just generated with one product photo and a prompt like "screengrab of a TikTok creator holding this product in her [kitchen / bathroom]" https://t.co/5SgRob4dhk

8) Novelty styles

There are some really fun templates to test in Gemini - they do all the work of prompting for the style
